items.summary: In FriendsModePage.tsx, add a persistent chat bar UI element positioned below the user's profile section. The bar must display a 'Friends' label or chat icon and be tappable/clickable to open a friends panel or drawer.
When the friends panel is opened and the user has no friends, display a clear 'No Friends' empty state message with a friendly prompt encouraging them to add friends.

When the user has one or more friends, display the friend list in the friends panel. Enforce a maximum friend limit of 1000 — if the user already has 1000 friends, prevent adding any more and show a 'Friend limit reached (1000/1000)' message. Store the friend relationships in the backend, associated with each user's principal.

Add a backend data model and functions for friend relationships: a FriendRequest record type with fields requesterId (Text), recipientId (Text), status (Text — 'pending', 'accepted', 'declined'), and sentAt (Int timestamp). Expose the following functions: sendFriendRequest(recipientPrincipal: Text) — creates a pending request; respondToFriendRequest(requesterId: Text, accept: Bool) — accepts or declines a request; getFriends() — returns all accepted friends of the calling principal; getFriendRequests() — returns all incoming pending requests for the calling principal. Enforce the 1000-friend cap in sendFriendRequest.

In the friends panel on FriendsModePage.tsx, display incoming pending friend requests as a separate section labelled 'Friend Requests'. Each pending request must show the requester's display name and two action buttons: 'Accept' (calls respondToFriendRequest with accept=true) and 'Decline' (calls respondToFriendRequest with accept=false). After accepting, the new friend appears in the friends list immediately.
